instruction_text_verbatim: For each word, the participants were asked to enter the age (in years) at which they thought they had learned the word. It was specified that by learning a word, "we mean the age at which you would have understood that word if somebody had used it in front of you, EVEN IF YOU DID NOT use, read, or write it at the time." When participants did not know a word, they were asked to enter the letter "x."
notes: Clear participant prompt quoted directly in Methods/Data collection.

instruction_text_verbatim: "Reading: When you are estimating your reading acquisition of a word, we mean the age at which you would have understood that word if you read it in print. Writing: When you are estimating written acquisition age, what we mean is when you estimate you could spell the word correctly".
notes: Direct participant-facing wording for reading/writing AoA.

instruction_text_verbatim: The purpose of this experiment is to rate words as to the ease with which a human body can physically interact with what they represent... Your body-object interaction ratings will be made on a 1 to 7 scale. A value of 1 will indicate a low body-object interaction rating, and a value of 7 will indicate a high body-object interaction rating.
notes: Full participant instructions included in appendix.

instruction_text_verbatim: We want you to indicate how concrete the meaning of each word is for you by using a 5-point rating scale going from abstract to concrete... Please indicate when you don't know a word by using the letter N (or n).
Then, they were asked to rate hundred words ranging from 1 (very feminine) to 7 (very masculine).
notes: Participant-facing scale endpoint wording reported explicitly; full instructions referenced in appendix.

instruction_text_verbatim: The rating scale ranges from 1 (humorless = not funny at all) to 5 (humorous = most funny)... Please work at a rapid pace and don't spend too much time thinking about each word. Rather, make your ratings based on your first and immediate reaction as you read each word.
notes: Direct instructions displayed in Data collection section.

instruction_text_verbatim: The same procedure and instructions were used for imageability and age of acquisition ratings as in Gilhooly and Logie (1980)... Verbs were shown in their infinitival form ... and nouns (where appropriate) were preceded by the indefinite article.
notes: Participant procedure/instruction description is explicit, with disambiguation rules.

instruction_text_verbatim: participants were asked to rate how much they experience everyday concepts using six perceptual senses/five action effectors ... the rating scales ran from 0 (not experienced at all with that sense/action) to 5 (experienced greatly with that sense/action); and that if participants did not know the meaning of a word, they should check the "Don't know the meaning of this word" box
notes: Participant-facing scale and 'don't know' instructions explicitly documented.

instruction_text_verbatim: In the lexical decision task (LDT), participants are presented with a string of letters (either a word or a nonword ...), and are asked to press one button if the string is a word and another button if the string is a nonword... In the speeded naming task, participants see a visual word ... and are asked to name the word aloud as quickly and as accurately as possible.
notes: Core participant task instructions reported directly in Methods/overview.

instruction_text_verbatim: For each letter string, participants had to indicate whether or not they knew the stimulus.
Please rate each word on a 1 to 7 scale, with 1 meaning the word evokes no sensory experience for you ... and 7 meaning the word evokes a strong sensory experience.
notes: Full participant instruction text is reproduced in appendix.

instruction_text_verbatim: participants were asked to rate the degree to which the words' meaning has social relevance by describing or referring to the following: a social characteristic of a person or group of people, a social behaviour or interaction, a social role, a social space, a social institution or system, a social value or ideology, or any other socially relevant concept.
notes: Directly reported participant-facing definition and rating criterion.

instruction_text_verbatim: The scale ranges from 1 (happy [excited; controlled]) to 9 (unhappy [calm; in control]) ... Please work at a rapid pace and don't spend too much time thinking about each word. Rather, make your ratings based on your first and immediate reaction as you read each word.
notes: Instruction text presented verbatim in Methods.

instruction_text_verbatim: Please select the pictures that you feel best represent the event described in the sentence, with your order of preference. Use 1 for the most preferred picture, 2 for the next preferred and so on. Please select a minimum of one picture for each sentence.
notes: Standalone participant instruction sheet with direct wording.

instruction_text_verbatim: Participants were instructed to choose at least one picture for each sentence and as many as they felt were applicable for the event that the sentence described. Participants were also instructed to rank their choices in order of preference, with 1 being the most preferred, 2 the next most preferred, and so on.
notes: Instruction wording is reported in Methods (not presented as full original participant script in this PDF). Full verbatim script appears in the separate Instructions.pdf.
